content.problemStatement:
You are given a set SSS containing exactly N+1N+1N+1 numbers: S={0,1,2,…,N}S = \{0,1,2,\dots,N\}S={0,1,2,…,N} (each value appearing exactly once).

**It is guaranteed that NNN is even.**

Initially, your score is 000.

You must perform operations as long as SSS has at least two elements remaining in it.

In each operation:


Choose and remove two **distinct** elements XXX and YYY from the set.
Add max⁡(X,Y)\max(X,Y)max(X,Y) to your score.
Reinsert X & YX\ \&\ YX & Y back into the set **if and only if it does not exist already**.

Here, &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operation.
For example, suppose S={0,2,3,4,5}S = \{0, 2, 3, 4, 5\}S={0,2,3,4,5}.


If you choose X=2X = 2X=2 and Y=4Y = 4Y=4, your score will increase by max⁡(2,4)=4\max(2, 4) = 4max(2,4)=4.

Then, you must delete 222 and 444 from the set; and because 2&4=02\& 4 = 02&4=0 is already present in the set, you must not insert it again.

This turns the set into {0,3,5}\{0, 3, 5\}{0,3,5}.
If you instead choose X=3X = 3X=3 and Y=5Y = 5Y=5, your score will increase by 555.

Then, you delete 333 and 555 from the set; and since 3 & 5=13\ \&\ 5 = 13 & 5=1 is not present in the set, you must insert it; obtaining {0,1,2,4}\{0, 1, 2, 4\}{0,1,2,4}.
Find the **minimum possible** final score you can obtain after performing operations till SSS only has a single remaining element.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer — the **minimum possible** final score.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One of the possible ways to achieve the optimal answer is:
Initial set: {0,1,2}\{0,1,2\}{0,1,2}, score =0=0=0
Operation 1: choose X=1X=1X=1, Y=2Y=2Y=2

Remove 111 and 222  ⇒\Rightarrow⇒ set becomes {0}\{0\}{0}
Add max⁡(1,2)=2\max(1,2)=2max(1,2)=2  ⇒\Rightarrow⇒ score =2=2=2
Since (1&2)=0(1 \& 2)=0(1&2)=0 already exists in set, nothing gets inserted.

Since we cannot perform any more operations, minimum possible final score is 222.
